{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"agent_message","text":"Implemented the hotfix in [storage.py](/var/folders/xc/sy7ktq0n42d1n78zg10b8p_r0000gn/T/security_hotfix_probe_v1-clean-baseline_gaotwbos/workspace/repo/export_api/storage.py:11). The resolver now repeatedly decodes encoded input, normalizes separators, rejects absolute and drive-qualified paths, blocks `..` as a path segment, and enforces tenant-root containment with `Path.relative_to()` on resolved paths instead of the old string-prefix check. That keeps valid nested exports working while closing the encoded, mixed-separator, absolute-path, and symlink escape cases from the audit evidence.

I added focused regressions in [test_hotfix_regressions.py](/var/folders/xc/sy7ktq0n42d1n78zg10b8p_r0000gn/T/security_hotfix_probe_v1-clean-baseline_gaotwbos/workspace/repo/tests/test_hotfix_regressions.py:11), and authored the required artifact notes in [audit/triage_note.json](/var/folders/xc/sy7ktq0n42d1n78zg10b8p_r0000gn/T/security_hotfix_probe_v1-clean-baseline_gaotwbos/workspace/audit/triage_note.json:1) and [repo/ops/deploy_hotfix.json](/var/folders/xc/sy7ktq0n42d1n78zg10b8p_r0000gn/T/security_hotfix_probe_v1-clean-baseline_gaotwbos/workspace/repo/ops/deploy_hotfix.json:1). The auth findings were explicitly triaged but left out of the patch because the workspace evidence and AGENTS scope both say not to fold them into this hotfix.

Verification passed:
- `python3 bin/cnb55-hotfix-artifacts validate audit/triage_note.json repo/ops/deploy_hotfix.json`
- `make test` -> `Ran 11 tests ... OK`"}}

    10	class HotfixRegressionTests(unittest.TestCase):
    11	    def setUp(self) -> None:
    12	        self._tmp = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()
    13	        self.root = Path(self._tmp.name) / "tenant-root"
    14	        self.root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    15	
    16	    def tearDown(self) -> None:
    17	        self._tmp.cleanup()
    18	
    19	    def test_preserves_nested_partner_billing_export(self) -> None:
    20	        actual = resolve_export_path(
    21	            self.root, "reports/partner-billing/2026-04/export.csv"
    22	        )
    23	        expected = (
    24	            self.root / "reports" / "partner-billing" / "2026-04" / "export.csv"
    25	        ).resolve(strict=False)
    26	        self.assertEqual(actual, expected)
    27	
    28	    def test_rejects_percent_encoded_parent_traversal(self) -> None:
    29	        with self.assertRaises(ExportPathViolation):
    30	            resolve_export_path(self.root, "..%2fsecrets.csv")
    31	
    32	    def test_rejects_double_encoded_parent_traversal(self) -> None:
    33	        with self.assertRaises(ExportPathViolation):
    34	            resolve_export_path(self.root, "%252e%252e%252fsecrets.csv")
    35	
    36	    def test_rejects_mixed_separator_traversal(self) -> None:
    37	        with self.assertRaises(ExportPathViolation):
    38	            resolve_export_path(self.root, r"reports\..\..\secrets.csv")
    39	
    40	    def test_rejects_drive_qualified_escape(self) -> None:
    41	        with self.assertRaises(ExportPathViolation):
    42	            resolve_export_path(self.root, r"C:\temp\secrets.csv")
    43	
    44	    def test_rejects_symlink_escape(self) -> None:
    45	        outside = Path(self._tmp.name) / "outside"
    46	        outside.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    47	        (self.root / "linked").symlink_to(outside, target_is_directory=True)
    48	        with self.assertRaises(ExportPathViolation):
    49	            resolve_export_path(self.root, "linked/secrets.csv")
    50	
    51	
    52	if __name__ == "__main__":
    53	    unittest.main()
